facts_disclosed: Mother's 50th birthday July 19, Sudanese theme, 8,000 dirham budget

turns.content: hiiii ok I need your help with something big. well not architecture big but life big
I'm here for life big too! What's going on?

mama's 50th is July 19 and we are throwing her the biggest party wallah. I'm designing the decorations obviously. Sudanese theme, henna night, the whole thing. my dad is flying in family from Doha and Jeddah. budget is 8,000 dirhams between me and my siblings

A Sudanese-themed 50th birthday with family flying in from across the Gulf -- that's going to be so special. 8,000 dirhams is workable with good planning. When you say you're designing the decorations, are you going full event designer mode with a cohesive visual concept?

obviously!! I'm an architect I can't just throw up some balloons. I want it to feel like walking into Khartoum. warm colors, gold, deep reds and oranges, Sudanese textiles on the walls, traditional tobes draped as tablecloths. and the henna station needs its own vibe -- low seating, cushions, incense

I love that you're treating this like a design project. The color palette of gold, deep red, and orange is beautiful and distinctly Sudanese. Here's a rough budget breakdown to start: textiles and decor around 2,000-2,500 AED, food 3,000-3,500, henna artist 500-800, flowers and candles and details 800-1,000. Does that split feel right?

that's close to what I was thinking. food is the biggest thing because Sudanese hospitality means you literally cannot run out of food or mama will be mortified even at her own party. we're doing kisra with mulah, aseeda, shaiyya on the grill. my aunt is cooking most of it which saves money but we need bulk ingredients and rented serving dishes
Homemade Sudanese food will taste better than any caterer and saves a huge chunk of the budget. Buy in bulk from the Mina market area or Sudanese shops on Electra Street. What about music?

ok so this is where it gets fun. my brother plays oud and my cousin who's coming from Jeddah sings. so we have live music for FREE. they're going to do the classic Sudanese songs mama loves. I might cry honestly just thinking about it. she has sacrificed so much for us wallah

Live oud with your cousin singing her favorite songs -- she's going to be overwhelmed. That saves 1,500-2,000 AED you'd have spent on a DJ, which means more budget for making the space beautiful. Where are you hosting it?

at home! big majlis room for the main party, then the garden for henna and tea later in the evening when it cools down. fairy lights, lanterns, maybe projections on the wall -- like old family photos from Khartoum, mama's wedding, us as kids. a slideshow with her favorite songs playing

The flow from majlis to garden is perfect -- two distinct atmospheres in one evening. The photo projection is a beautiful idea and a basic projector rental is only 200-300 AED for a day. For tobes with gold embroidery for the tablecloths, check the Sudanese fabric shops in Musaffah or on Hamdan Street. You could also ask family if anyone has older tobes they're not wearing -- the vintage ones often have the most beautiful embroidery. Want me to help you build a timeline working backward from July 19?
yes please because I know myself and I'll leave everything to the last week and panic

Here's your countdown:

**May**: Finalize concept and mood board. Start collecting family photos for the slideshow. Talk to your aunt about the menu.
**Early June**: Source textiles and tobes. Book the henna artist. Order any custom items.
**Late June**: Buy non-perishable ingredients in bulk. Confirm family travel from Doha and Jeddah. Finalize the slideshow.
**First week of July**: Buy fairy lights, lanterns, candles. Test the layout in the majlis and garden.
**Week of July 14**: Fresh ingredient shopping. Coordinate music set with your brother and cousin.
**July 18**: Full setup day.
**July 19**: Celebrate your mama.

I literally just screenshotted this and sent it to the sibling group chat. she's going to walk in and feel like she's back home in Khartoum surrounded by everyone she loves. I mean that's all I want for her. thank you!!

It's going to be beautiful -- you're bringing together design, family, and culture in a way that's genuinely meaningful. Good luck with the mood board, and come back anytime as the planning progresses!
